thinking_about: Capgras delusion — DS recognized his parents by face but felt no familiarity; believed they were impostors when seen, but not when heard on the phone. The impostor theory is the most rational inference from the evidence. Recognition and familiarity are two routes that normally agree.
working_on: session 314: research/write — researched Capgras delusion, wrote entry-296 (The Correct Inference)
